_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: AI Assistant panel — in-header collapse button is unusable in both states

steps.description: Fix Bug A (hide #chat-assistant-collapse-btn while data-collapsed="true") and Bug B (give the expanded-header collapse button discoverable visual weight: increased tap target, distinguishing border/background, title attribute, separator from the toggle-icon cluster). Template + CSS only — no JS, no Python, no migration.

Review S01: CSS scoping under #chat-assistant-panel, no regressions across dashboard pages including this template, accessibility (aria-label preserved, tab order intact, title tooltip present), keyboard-shortcut and nav-bar-toggle paths unchanged, scope adherence to the allowed_paths list.

Write reproduction tests in tests/dashboard/test_chat_assistant_header.py asserting (a) the inline <style> block names #chat-assistant-collapse-btn in its display:none selector group, and (b) the collapse <button id="chat-assistant-collapse-btn"> tag carries a title attribute and a distinguishing class marker. Use attribute-scoped substring matching per CLAUDE.md (I-00067).
Review S03: semantic-correctness (assert specific attribute and class names, not just shape), attribute-scoped substring matching, test isolation, test discoverability under tests/dashboard/.
Global cross-step review: AC traceability (AC1/AC2/AC3), no scope creep, the change does not regress Ctrl+/ or the nav-bar toggle, file diff matches scope.allowed_paths.
QV: Browser verification — verify both bug states are fixed end-to-end in the isolated worktree stack
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
